title: Resolve-before-context vs. agentic round-trip benchmark

structural_roundtrip_model.note: Round-trip counts are an architectural property derived from the real directive manifest, NOT a timed live-model measurement. In an agentic tool-calling design the model performs one round-trip per context-gathering operation plus a final synthesis call; the resolve-before-context pipeline resolves all directives in one pre-model pass and issues exactly one model call.

patent_linkage: Supports the §101 technical-effect narrative: deterministic single-pass resolution yields one model round-trip instead of N, with a byte-reproducible, auditable assembled context.
